LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LEADING INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO PLAINTIFF IBRAHIM MOHAMMED ("MOHAMMED") TO ALLEGE FACTS THAT SUPPORT THAT HE IS A THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY; OVERRULED AS TO PLAINTIFF MI PUEBLITO MARKET, LLC ("LLC"). AS TO FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION AS TO MOHAMMED, SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR THE SAME GROUNDS AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION; AS TO LLC, SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION AS A SINGLE NON-DUPLICATIVE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS PLAINTIFFS HAVE WITHDRAWN THE CAUSE OF ACTION. AS TO THE FIFTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO PLEAD ADDITIONAL FACTS TO MEET THE PARTICULARITY STANDARD FOR A CAUSE OF ACTION OF FRAUD. (SEE LAZAR V. SUPERIOR COURT (1996) 12 CAL.4TH 631, 645 ("HOW, WHEN, WHERE, TO WHOM, AND BY WHAT MEANS").) AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS EXEMPLARY DAMAGES ARE A REMEDY, NOT A CAUSE OF ACTION, AND THE FACTS SUPPORTING EXEMPLARY DAMAGES SHOULD BE PLED IN THE CHARGING ALLEGATIONS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185 (302/MJM)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LEADING INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Overruled in part and sustained in part with 10 days leave to amend. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. As to the third cause of action, sustained with leave to amend as to Plaintiff Ibrahim Mohammed ("Mohammed") to allege facts that support that he is a third party beneficiary; overruled as to Plaintiff Mi Pueblito Market, LLC ("LLC"). As to first and second causes of action as to Mohammed, sustained with leave to amend for the same grounds as to the third cause of action; as to LLC, sustained with leave to amend to plead the first and second causes of action as a single non-duplicative cause of action for breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. As to the fourth cause of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as Plaintiffs have withdrawn the cause of action. As to the fifth and seventh causes of action, Plaintiffs are given 10 days leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to plead additional facts to meet the particularity standard for a cause of action of fraud. (See Lazar v. Superior Court (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645 ("how, when, where, to whom, and by what means").) As to the sixth cause of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as exemplary damages are a remedy, not a cause of action, and the facts supporting exemplary damages should be pled in the charging allegations. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: E. Larnauti; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185 (302/MJM)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Compl
Off Calendar Sep-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF IBRAHIM MOHAMMED's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint is
Granted, in part. Within 10 days, Plaintiff may file his First Amended Complaint. Defendant does not oppose the motion as a matter of legal principle. (Opposition, p. 1:24-25.) To the extent that Defendant qualifies its stipulation by opposing some of the proposed allegations, Defendant may file a demurrer or other motion to challenge the allegations of the First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff's request that the proposed First Amended Complaint be deemed filed and served upon the granting of the instant motion is denied. Plaintiff must actually file and serve the First Amended Complaint. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.)
